perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, Shipper has requested Transporter to transport Gas on its behalf and Transporter represents that it is willing to transport Gas under the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, Shipper has requested Transporter to transport Gas on its behalf; however, Transporter's willingness to transport Gas is subject to operational capacity and other pre-existing contractual obligations, and Transporter represents that it is willing to transport Gas under the terms and conditions of this Agreement to the best of its abilities.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity to the Transporter's obligation. The original text stated a clear willingness, whereas the modification introduces conditions ('subject to operational capacity and other pre-existing contractual obligations') and softens the commitment ('to the best of its abilities'). This contradicts the original intent of a firm transportation agreement and paragraph 8 which specifies a Maximum Daily Quantity that implies a guaranteed transport volume. This creates an in-text contradiction since the MDQ is less definite now.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. MAXIMUM DAILY QUANTITY (Dth/Day): 101,300 Please see Appendix A for further detail.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces conditions ('subject to operational capacity and other pre-existing contractual obligations') and softens the commitment ('to the best of its abilities') regarding the Transporter's willingness to transport Gas. This directly contradicts paragraph 8 which specifies a Maximum Daily Quantity (MDQ) of 101,300 Dth/Day. The MDQ implies a guaranteed transport volume, while the changed text suggests that the transporter might not be able to meet that volume, creating ambiguity and a potential conflict of obligations. Therefore, a clear in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Unless Shipper and Transporter have agreed to a rate other than the maximum rate, rates shall be Transporter's maximum rates and charges plus all applicable surcharges in effect from time to time under the applicable Rate Schedule (as stated above) on file with the Commission unless otherwise agreed to by the parties in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless Shipper and Transporter have agreed to a rate other than the maximum rate, rates shall be Transporter's currently published rates and charges plus all applicable surcharges in effect from time to time under any applicable Rate Schedule on file with the Commission or otherwise determined by the Transporter, unless specifically agreed to by the parties in writing. The Transporter reserves the right to modify its rates, charges, and surcharges at any time, without prior notice to the Shipper.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specified that rates would be the 'Transporter's maximum rates' unless otherwise agreed. The modified text allows the 'Transporter's currently published rates' to be used and gives Transporter right to modify the rates anytime, contradicting the stability implied by referencing a specific rate schedule and requiring written agreement for deviations. The added sentence contradicts the established mechanism for setting rates, therefore creating an internal contradiction. This change has implications for paragraph 12, specifically for 'General Terms and Conditions' which become effective by operation of law and Commission Order.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. INCORPORATION OF TARIFF INTO AGREEMENT: This Agreement shall incorporate and in all respects be subject to the "General Terms and Conditions" and the applicable Rate Schedule (as stated above) set forth in Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff, Third Revised Volume No. 1, as may be revised from time to time. Transporter may file and seek Commission approval under Section 4 of the Natural Gas Act (NGA) at any time and from time to time to change any rates, charges or provisions set forth in the applicable Rate Schedule (as stated above) and the "General Terms and Conditions" in Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff, Third Revised Volume No. 1, and Transporter shall have the right to place such changes in effect in accordance with the NGA, and this Agreement shall be deemed to include such changes and any such changes Source: TC PIPELINES LP, 10-K, 2/26/2016 which become effective by operation of law and Commission Order, without prejudice to Shipper's right to protest the same.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants the Transporter the unilateral right to modify rates at any time without prior notice. This directly contradicts paragraph 12, which incorporates the applicable Rate Schedule from the Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff and implies that changes to rates, charges, or provisions require Commission approval and are subject to the NGA. The ability to change rates at any time without notice undermines the established mechanism for rate changes outlined in paragraph 12, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any controversy between the parties arising under this Agreement and not resolved by the parties shall be determined in accordance with the laws of the State of Michigan.
perturbation.changed_text: Any controversy between the parties arising under this Agreement and not resolved by the parties shall be subject to non-binding mediation in Houston, Texas, before being determined in accordance with the laws of the State of Michigan.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandated that any unresolved controversies be settled in accordance with Michigan law. The modified text inserts a mandatory step of non-binding mediation in Houston, Texas, before Michigan law can be applied. This creates an in-text contradiction as it is unclear whether the controversy is determined after Michigan laws, or is it the outcome of the mediation. The additional step introduces a jurisdictional and procedural ambiguity, especially if mediation fails. This affects paragraph 15, particularly how to proceed with 'NOTICES AND COMMUNICATIONS'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15. NOTICES AND COMMUNICATIONS: All notices and communications with respect to this Agreement shall be in writing by mail, e-mail, or fax, or other means as agreed to by the parties, and sent to the addresses stated below or to any other such address(es) as may be designated in writing by mail, e-mail, or fax, or other means similarly agreed to: ADMINISTRATIVE MATTERS Great Lakes Gas Transmission Limited Partnership Commercial Services 700 Louisiana St., Suite 700 Houston, TX 77002-2700 ANR PIPELINE COMPANY 700 Louisiana St., Suite 700 Houston, TX 77002-2700 Attn: AGREED TO BY: GREAT LAKES GAS TRANSMISSION LIMITED PARTNERSHIP By: Great Lakes Gas Transmission Company ANR PIPELINE COMPANY /s/ STEVEN D. JASKOLSKI Steven D. Jaskolski Director, Commercial Services /s/ JOSEPH E. POLLARD Joseph E. Pollard Director, Long Term Marketing Dated: December 14, 2015 Dated: December 14, 2015 Source: TC PIPELINES LP, 10-K, 2/26/2016
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a mandatory non-binding mediation in Houston, Texas, prior to the application of Michigan law. This contradicts paragraph 15, which stipulates that notices and communications regarding the agreement should be sent to addresses in Houston, Texas, potentially related to administrative matters and implicitly suggesting that all communications regarding the agreement must be directed to Houston, Texas. The introduction of mediation in Houston adds another layer of jurisdictional complexity that wasn't present in the original agreement and complicates the procedure described in paragraph 15.
